evidence_quotes.quote: problematic features of digital products such as in video games
especially if it tied to online services.
Will the game be partially operable or does that make it fully unplayable?

paragraphs.text: Hi, the "Digital Fairness Act" (DFA) is a needed initiative, just as "Stop Destroying Videogames" (SVG). We currently don't know if the SVG initiative does produce results that the gamers are at least complacent with, but since the public informationon DFA mentions "problematic features of digital products such as in video games" then DFA should touch on the subject what happens to games when they become irrevelent to the game publisher, especially if it tied to online services. Will the game be partially operable or does that make it fully unplayable? Especially if the game has been sold to the customer as a product, not issued a licence.
